column2: Which three decoupling dimensions are characteristic of publish/subscribe systems?
In topic-based pub/sub, a publication matches a subscription when …
column3: The publication topic list is a superset of the subscription topic list
column4: The intersection of publication and subscription topic lists is non-empty
column5: The subscription topic list is exactly equal to the publication topic list
column6: The publication has at least one topic not in the subscription list

column2: In subject-based pub/sub (TIBCO/Rendezvous style), a subscription key/interval entry matches a publication when …
column3: The publication contains that key and its value lies within the subscription interval
column4: The publication contains that key regardless of the value
column5: The subscription interval spans the entire real line
column6: The publication omits that key entirely

column2: What does it mean for predicate p1 to *cover* predicate p2 in CBCB content-based routing?
column3: p1 and p2 select exactly the same set of messages
column4: Every message selected by p2 is also selected by p1 (p1 is more general)
column5: Every message selected by p1 is also selected by p2 (p2 is more general)
column6: p1 and p2 are syntactically identical

column2: A strict publish/subscribe system (with *no memory*) behaves as follows regarding late subscribers:
column3: Late subscribers receive all publications that occurred before they subscribed
column4: Late subscribers receive only publications that occur after they subscribe
column5: Late subscribers receive a configurable number of past publications
column6: Late subscribers are rejected until the backlog is cleared

column2: In 0mq pub/sub, where does topic filtering happen?

column3: A full mesh connecting all N servers

column5: A ring topology with redundant links
column6: A random graph for fault tolerance

column2: Which pub/sub variant matches publications by inspecting their *actual payload* rather than attached metadata?

column3: It provides stronger delivery guarantees than needed
column4: It does not address dependability or load balancing

column3: Topics; content-based subscriptions using SQL-like predicates
